              }\n            };\n        }\n\n        public IEnumerable<Player> GetAllPlayers()\n        {\n            return _players.AsReadOnly();\n        }\n\n        public Player? GetPlayerById(int playerId)\n        {\n            return _players.FirstOrDefault(p => p.PlayerId == playerId);\n        }\n\n        public Player? GetPlayerByUsername(string username)\n        {\n            return _players.FirstOrDefault(p => p.Username.Equals(username, StringComparison.OrdinalIgnoreCase));\n        }\n\n        public IEnumerable<Character> GetCharactersByPlayerId(int playerId)\n        {\n            var player = GetPlayerById(playerId);\n            return player?.CharacterList ?? new List<Character>();\n        }\n\n        public Character? GetCharacterById(int characterId)\n        {\n            return _players\n                .SelectMany(p => p.CharacterList)\n                .FirstOrDefault(c => c.CharacterId == characterId);\n        }\n\n        public void AddPlayer(Player player)\n        {\n            if (_players.Any(p => p.PlayerId == player.PlayerId))\n            {\n                throw new InvalidOperationException($\"Player with ID {player.PlayerId} already exists.\");\n            }\n\n            _players.Add(player);\n        }\n\n        public void AddCharacter(int playerId, Character character)\n        {\n            var player = GetPlayerById(playerId);\n            if (player == null)\n            {\n                throw new InvalidOperationException($\"Player with ID {playerId} not found.\");\n            }\n\n            if (player.CharacterList.Any(c => c.CharacterId == character.CharacterId))\n            {\n                throw new InvalidOperationException($\"Character with ID {character.CharacterId} already exists for this player.\");\n            }\n\n            player.CharacterList.Add(character);\n        }\n\n        public void UpdatePlayer(Player player)\n        {\n            var existingPlayer = GetPlayerById(player.PlayerId);\n            if (existingPlayer == null)\n            {\n                throw new InvalidOperationException($\"Player with ID {player.PlayerId} not found.\");\n            }\n\n            existingPlayer.Name = player.Name;\n            existingPlayer.Surname = player.Surname;\n            existingPlayer.Username = player.Username;\n            existingPlayer.Email = player.Email;\n            existingPlayer.Password = player.Password;\n            existingPlayer.LastLogin = player.LastLogin;\n        }\n\n        public bool DeletePlayer(int playerId)\n        {\n            var player = GetPlayerById(playerId);\n            if (player == null)\n            {\n                return false;\n            }\n\n            return _players.Remove(player);\n        }\n\n        public bool DeleteCharacter(int characterId)\n        {\n            foreach (var player in _players)\n            {\n                var character = player.CharacterList.FirstOrDefault(c => c.CharacterId == characterId);\n                if (character != null)\n                {\n                    return player.CharacterList.Remove(character);\n                }\n            }\n\n            return false;\n        }\n {"timestamp":"2026-05-18T22:56:40.026Z","hook_event_name":"PreToolUse","session_id":"3793e7a4-37f1-4b0e-8952-82d937b93915","transcript_path":"c:\\Users\\David Laptop\\AppData\\Roaming\\Code\\User\\workspaceStorage\\67336a66a4a40a4161dc2c41d57a767b\\GitHub.copilot-chat\\transcripts\\3793e7a4-37f1-4b0e-8952-82d937b93915.jsonl","tool_name":"create_file","tool_input":{"filePath":"c:\\TVZ\\Druga_apsolventska\\ASP_Net\\Projekt\\DnD_Character_Sheet_Creator\\.github\\skills\\edit-form\\SKILL.md","content":"---\nname: edit-form\ndescription: \"Use when: creating or updating a Create/Edit form page for ASP.NET Core Models. Includes ViewModel, controller actions, and Razor view with validation.\"\n---\n\n# Edit/Create Form Page Builder\n\nCreates a complete Create/Edit form workflow for D&D Character Sheet entities (Players, Characters). Produces: ViewModel, controller actions (GET/POST), and form view with client/server validation.\n\n## Workflow\n\n### 1. Create/Update ViewModel\n- Located in `DnD_Character_Sheet_Creator.Web/ViewModels/<EntityName>FormViewModel.cs`\n- Include all editable properties from the entity model\n- Add `[Display]` attributes for labels\n- Add `[Required]`, `[Range]`, `[StringLength]` validation attributes\n- Include nested collections (e.g., list of available options for dropdowns)\n- Example: `PlayerFormViewModel` with Name, Surname, and list of available Classes\n\n**Checklist:**\n- [ ] Properties match entity fields\n- [ ] All validation attributes applied\n- [ ] Display names are user-friendly\n- [ ] Collection properties for dropdowns exist\n\n### 2. Controller Actions\nAdd to appropriate controller (PlayersController, CharactersController):\n\n**GET Action (Display Form)**\n- Route: `[HttpGet] [Route(\"Create\")]` for new records or `[HttpGet] [Route(\"Edit/{id}\")]` for existing\n- Fetch entity by ID (if editing)\n- Populate ViewModel with entity data or defaults\n- Load collection properties (dropdown options) from repository\n- Return view with populated ViewModel\n- Handle missing entity with `NotFound()`\n\n**POST Action (Process Form)**\n- Route: `[HttpPost]` matching the GET route\n- Accept ViewModel parameter\n- Validate `ModelState.IsValid`\n- Map ViewModel to entity (use AutoMapper or manual mapping)\n- Call repository method: `_repository.AddPlayer()` or `_repository.UpdatePlayer()`\n- Redirect to Details or Index on success\n- Return view with errors on validation failure\n\n**Checklist:**\n- [ ] Both GET and POST actions created\n- [ ] ModelState validation in POST\n- [ ] Repository methods called correctly\n- [ ] Redirect to appropriate page after save\n- [ ] NotFound() for missing entities in Edit\n\n### 3. Create View (`Views/<Entity>/Create.cshtml` or `Edit.cshtml`)\n- Inherit ViewModel: `@model PlayerFormViewModel`\n- Add form with `asp-action` and `asp-controller` attributes\n- Use Razor Tag Helpers for input binding:\n  - `<input asp-for=\"PropertyName\" />` for text inputs\n  - `<select asp-for=\"PropertyName\" asp-items=\"@Model.Options\"></select>` for dropdowns\n  - `<textarea asp-for=\"PropertyName\"></textarea>` for longer text\n- Add validation messages: `<span asp-validation-for=\"PropertyName\"></span>`\n- Include jQuery Validation scripts in view or layout\n- Add Submit button with action name\n\n**Form Elements by Property Type:**\n| Entity Field | ViewModel Property | Form Element | Validation |\n|---|---|---|---|\n| String (name, email) | `string Name` | `<input asp-for=\"Name\" />` | Required, StringLength |\n| String (long text) | `string Biography` | `<textarea asp-for=\"Biography\" />` | StringLength, Display |\n| Number (level, age) | `int Level` | `<input asp-for=\"Level\" type=\"number\" />` | Required, Range |\n| Enum (Class, Race) | `ClassEnum SelectedClass` | `<select asp-for=\"SelectedClass\" asp-items=\"@Model.ClassOptions\">` | Required |\n| Bool | `bool IsActive` | `<input asp-for=\"IsActive\" type=\"checkbox\" />` | None |\n| DateTime | `DateTime CreatedDate` | `<input asp-for=\"CreatedDate\" type=\"datetime-local\" />` | Required |\n\n**Checklist:**\n- [ ] View matches ViewModel type\n- [ ] All ViewModel properties have form elements\n- [ ] Validation messages displayed for each field\n- [ ] Submit button targets correct action\n- [ ] Cancel/Back link provided\n- [ ] Form layout uses Bootstrap classes consistent with site\n\n### 4. Add Validation & Error Handling\n- **Server-side:** Check `ModelState.IsValid` in POST action\n- **Client-side:** jQuery Validation & Unobtrusive enabled in wwwroot\n  - Included via `wwwroot/lib/jquery-validation/`\n  - Script references in Shared Layout: `_Layout.cshtml`\n- **Custom Validation:** Create `[CustomValidation]` attributes in ViewModel if needed\n  - Example: Ensure character level Gëń player level\n\n**Checklist:**\n- [ ] ModelState checked in POST action\n- [ ] Validation messages appear on client\n- [ ] Submit disabled until valid (optional)\n- [ ] Server validates even if client-side bypassed\n- [ ] Validation error messages are user-friendly\n\n### 5. Update Index/Details Views with Links\n- Add \"Create\" button on Index page\n- Add \"Edit\" link on Details or Index rows\n- Add \"Delete\" link if applicable (optional)\n- Use button helpers: `<a asp-action=\"Create\" asp-controller=\"Players\" class=\"btn btn-primary\">New Player</a>`\n\n**Checklist:**\n- [ ] Create button on Index\n- [ ] Edit link points to correct route with ID\n- [ ] Links use appropriate Bootstrap button styles\n- [ ] Links are visually discoverable\n\n## Decision Points\n\n**Shared vs. Separate Views?**\n- If Create and Edit forms are identical GĺĆ Use single view, route both GET actions to it\n- If forms differ (e.g., Edit hides created date) GĺĆ Create separate `Create.cshtml` and `Edit.cshtml`\n- Common: Use shared view, conditionally hide fields with `@if (Model.CharacterId == 0) { }`\n\n**Enum Dropdowns?**\n- Load from ViewModel collection: `asp-items=\"@Model.ClassOptions\"`\n- Populate in controller using `Enum.GetValues()` or hardcoded list\n- Example: `Model.ClassOptions = Enum.GetNames(typeof(ClassEnum)).Select(x => new SelectListItem { Text = x, Value = x })`\n\n**Nested Properties (e.g., Equipment)?**\n- If simple: Include in single form (Equipment fields in Character form)\n- If complex: Redirect to separate Equipment management page\n- Current approach: Focus on primary entity, link to nested entities separately\n\n## Template Files\n\nUse these as starting points:\n\n### PlayerFormViewModel.cshtml\n```csharp\nusing System.ComponentModel.DataAnnotations;\nusing Microsoft.AspNetCore.Mvc.Rendering;\n\nnamespace DnD_Character_Sheet_Creator.Web.ViewModels\n{\n    public class PlayerFormViewModel\n    {\n        public int PlayerId { get; set; }\n        \n        [Required(ErrorMessage = \"First name is required\")]\n        [StringLength(50)]\n        [Display(Name = \"First Name\")]\n        public string Name { get; set; }\n\n        [Required(ErrorMessage = \"Last name is required\")]\n        [StringLength(50)]\n        [Display(Name = \"Last Name\")]\n        public string Surname { get; set; }\n\n        [Display(Name = \"Biography\")]\n        [StringLength(500)]\n        public string Biography { get; set; }\n\n        // For dropdowns\n        public List<SelectListItem> AvailableClasses { get; set; } = new();\n    }\n}\n```\n\n### Controller Pattern\n```csharp\n[HttpGet]\n[Route(\"Create\")]\npublic IActionResult Create()\n{\n    var viewModel = new PlayerFormViewModel \n    { \n        AvailableClasses = GetClassOptions() \n    };\n    return View(viewModel);\n}\n\n[HttpPost]\n[Route(\"Create\")]\npublic IActionResult Create(PlayerFormViewModel viewModel)\n{\n    if (!ModelState.IsValid)\n    {\n        viewModel.AvailableClasses = GetClassOptions();\n        return View(viewModel);\n    }\n\n    var player = new Player \n    { \n        Name = viewModel.Name, \n        Surname = viewModel.Surname \n    };\n    _playerRepository.AddPlayer(player);\n    return RedirectToAction(\"Details\", new { id = player.PlayerId });\n}\n\nprivate List<SelectListItem> GetClassOptions()\n{\n    return Enum.GetNames(typeof(ClassEnum))\n        .Select(x => new SelectListItem { Text = x, Value = x })\n        .ToList();\n}\n```\n\n### View Pattern (Create.cshtml)\n```html\n@model PlayerFormViewModel\n\n@{\n    ViewData[\"Title\"] = \"Create Player\";\n}\n\n<div class=\"container mt-5\">\n    <div class=\"row justify-content-center\">\n        <div class=\"col-md-6\">\n            <h2>@ViewData[\"Title\"]</h2>\n            \n            <form asp-action=\"Create\" asp-controller=\"Players\" method=\"post\">\n                <div class=\"form-group mb-3\">\n                    <label asp-for=\"Name\"></label>\n                    <input asp-for=\"Name\" class=\"form-control\" />\n                    <span asp-validation-for=\"Name\" class=\"text-danger\"></span>\n                </div>\n\n                <div class=\"form-group mb-3\">\n                    <label asp-for=\"Surname\"></label>\n                    <input asp-for=\"Surname\" class=\"form-control\" />\n                    <span asp-validation-for=\"Surname\" class=\"text-danger\"></span>\n                </div>\n\n                <div class=\"form-group mb-3\">\n                    <label asp-for=\"Biography\"></label>\n                    <textarea asp-for=\"Biography\" class=\"form-control\"></textarea>\n                    <span asp-validation-for=\"Biography\" class=\"text-danger\"></span>\n                </div>\n\n                <div class=\"form-group\">\n                    <button type=\"submit\" class=\"btn btn-primary\">Create</button>\n                    <a asp-action=\"Index\" class=\"btn btn-secondary\">Cancel</a>\n                </div>\n            </form>\n        </div>\n    </div>\n</div>\n\n@section Scripts {\n    @{await Html.RenderPartialAsync(\"_ValidationScriptsPartial\");}\n}\n```\n\n## Quality Checklist\n\n- [ ] ViewModel in `ViewModels/` folder with clear name\n- [ ] Validation attributes on all user-editable fields\n- [ ] Controller GET displays form with populated dropdowns\n- [ ] Controller POST validates, maps, saves, and redirects\n- [ ] View uses `asp-for` Tag Helpers (not hardcoded names)\n- [ ] All form fields have error message spans\n- [ ] Bootstrap styling applied consistently\n- [ ] Index/Details views have create/edit links\n- [ ] Form tested with valid data GĺĆ saves GŁô\n- [ ] Form tested with invalid data GĺĆ shows errors GŁô\n- [ ] Form tested with missing entity (Edit) GĺĆ NotFound GŁô\n\n## Related Skills\n- Character equipment management (nested forms)\n- Form styling & DnD theme Produces a ViewModel, controller GET/POST actions, and a Razor view with validation and consistent project styling.\n+\n+## Workflow\n+\n+### 1. Define the Create ViewModel\n+- Place it in `DnD_Character_Sheet_Creator.Web/ViewModels/<EntityName>FormViewModel.cs`\n+- Include only fields needed to create the new record\n+- Mark required fields with `[Required]`\n+- Add `[StringLength]`, `[Range]`, `[EmailAddress]`, and other validation attributes where appropriate\n+- Add friendly labels with `[Display(Name = \"...\")]`\n+- Include any dropdown sources as lists, select lists, or enum option collections\n+\n+**Checklist:**\n+- [ ] ViewModel contains only create-time fields\n+- [ ] Validation attributes match the model rules\n+- [ ] Display labels are user-friendly\n+- [ ] Dropdown or enum option data is available when needed\n+\n+### 2. Add Controller Actions\n+Add the Create actions to the relevant controller, usually `PlayersController` or `CharactersController`.\n+\n+**GET Create**\n+- Use `[HttpGet]` and `[Route(\"Create\")]`\n+- Instantiate the ViewModel\n+- Populate dropdown or enum option collections\n+- Return the view with the prepared ViewModel\n+\n+**POST Create**\n+- Use `[HttpPost]` and `[Route(\"Create\")]`\n+- Accept the same ViewModel type\n+- Check `ModelState.IsValid`\n+- Rebuild dropdown or enum option collections before returning the view on validation failure\n+- Map the ViewModel to the entity\n+- Set any defaults that should exist only at creation time, such as timestamps or initial values\n+- Save through the repository\n+- Redirect to `Details` or `Index` after success\n+\n+**Checklist:**\n+- [ ] GET action prepares the form data\n+- [ ] POST action validates input before saving\n+- [ ] Form data is repopulated when validation fails\n+- [ ] Entity is saved through the repository\n+- [ ] Success redirects go to the right page\n+\n+### 3. Build the Razor View\n+- Create `Views/<Entity>/Create.cshtml`\n+- Use `@model <EntityName>FormViewModel`\n+- Follow the projectGÇÖs card-based layout with `sheet-card`, `reveal`, and Bootstrap spacing\n+- Bind inputs with `asp-for`\n+- Show validation messages with `asp-validation-for`\n+- Use `type=\"email\"`, `type=\"number\"`, or `textarea` when the field type requires it\n+- Include a submit button and a cancel/back link\n+- Add `@section Scripts` with `_ValidationScriptsPartial`\n+\n+**Checklist:**\n+- [ ] View matches the ViewModel type\n+- [ ] Every editable field has a form control\n+- [ ] Validation messages are visible under each field\n+- [ ] Cancel link returns to a sensible page\n+- [ ] Validation scripts are included\n+\n+### 4. Add Entry Points\n+- Add a Create button on the entity Index page\n+- If useful, add a Create link in the navigation or details page\n+- Point links to the controllerGÇÖs `Create` action\n+\n+**Checklist:**\n+- [ ] Users can discover the Create page from the list view\n+- [ ] Link targets the correct controller and action\n+- [ ] Button styling matches the rest of the site\n+\n+## Decision Points\n+\n+**Does the entity need dropdowns?**\n+- If yes, load the options in the GET action and rebuild them in the POST action on validation failure\n+- If the option set is small and fixed, an enum-backed select list is fine\n+- If the option set comes from another repository, load it from that repository in the controller\n+\n+**Does the entity need defaults on create?**\n+- If yes, set them in the POST action before saving\n+- Examples include timestamps, initial levels, or empty child collections\n+- Keep edit-only values out of the create form unless the user must choose them explicitly\n+\n+**Should Create share a view with Edit?**\n+- If the fields are identical, a shared form can reduce duplication\n+- If create needs fewer fields or different defaults, keep a dedicated Create view\n+- For this project, prefer a separate Create page when it keeps the workflow clearer\n+\n+## Template Pattern\n+\n+### Controller Pattern\n+```csharp\n+[HttpGet]\n+[Route(\"Create\")]\n+public IActionResult Create()\n+{\n+    var viewModel = new PlayerFormViewModel();\n+    return View(viewModel);\n+}\n+\n+[HttpPost]\n+[Route(\"Create\")]\n+public IActionResult Create(PlayerFormViewModel viewModel)\n+{\n+    if (!ModelState.IsValid)\n+    {\n+        return View(viewModel);\n+    }\n+\n+    var player = new Player\n+    {\n+        Name = viewModel.Name,\n+        Surname = viewModel.Surname,\n+        Username = viewModel.Username,\n+        Email = viewModel.Email\n+    };\n+\n+    _playerRepository.AddPlayer(player);\n+    return RedirectToAction(\"Details\", new { id = player.PlayerId });\n+}\n+```\n+\n+### View Pattern\n+```html\n+@model PlayerFormViewModel\n+\n+@{\n+    ViewData[\"Title\"] = \"Create Player\";\n+}\n+\n+<div class=\"sheet-card mb-4 reveal\">\n+    <p class=\"sheet-kicker mb-1\">New Entry</p>\n+    <h1 class=\"h3 asp-validation-for=\"Name\" class=\"text-danger small d-block mt-1\"></span>\n+                    </div>\n+\n+                    <div class=\"mb-4\">\n+                        <label asp-for=\"Surname\" class=\"form-label\"></label>\n+                        <input asp-for=\"Surname\" class=\"form-control\" placeholder=\"Last name\" />\n+                        <span asp-validation-for=\"Surname\" class=\"text-danger small d-block mt-1\"></span>\n+                    </div>\n+\n+                    <div class=\"mb-4\">\n+                        <label asp-for=\"Email\" class=\"form-label\"></label>\n+                        <input asp-for=\"Email\" class=\"form-control\" type=\"email\" placeholder=\"Email address\" />\n+                        <span asp-validation-for=\"Email\" class=\"text-danger small d-block mt-1\"></span>\n+                    </div>\n+\n+                    <div class=\"d-flex gap-2\">\n+                        <button type=\"submit\" class=\"btn btn-primary\">Create</button>\n+                        <a asp-action=\"Index\" class=\"btn btn-outline-ink\">Cancel</a>\n+                    </div>\n+ Produces a confirmation page, controller actions, repository updates, and list filtering so removed records are hidden from normal entity lists.\n\n## Workflow\n\n### 1. Add Soft-Delete Support to the Entity\n- Add a nullable `DeletedAt` property to every entity that can be removed\n- Use `DateTime? DeletedAt` so active entities stay visible and removed entities can be marked with a timestamp\n- Add or update the EF Core migration and model snapshot if the property does not already exist\n- Keep the existing primary key and domain fields unchanged\n\n**Checklist:**\n- [ ] Entity has a nullable `DeletedAt` property\n- [ ] Migration adds the column for all affected entities\n- [ ] Existing data remains valid after the schema update\n\n### 2. Update Repository Logic\n- Change delete operations to perform a soft delete instead of removing rows\n- Set `DeletedAt = DateTime.UtcNow` or the projectGÇÖs chosen timestamp standard when the object is removed\n- Update repository query methods so normal reads ignore rows where `DeletedAt` is not null\n- If the repository exposes a details or lookup method, decide whether it should return removed rows or hide them consistently with the rest of the app\n\n**Checklist:**\n- [ ] Delete methods set `DeletedAt` instead of calling hard delete\n- [ ] Read methods filter out removed entities by default\n- [ ] Repository behavior is consistent across list, details, and edit flows\n\n### 3. Build the Remove Confirmation Page\n- Create `Views/<Entity>/Remove.cshtml` or `Delete.cshtml` depending on the existing naming in the controller\n- Use the page to confirm the user wants to remove the object before applying the soft delete\n- Show the key identifying fields only, enough for the user to confirm the target record\n- Use the existing parchment/card styling and the project button conventions\n- Include a primary remove button and a cancel/back link\n\n**Checklist:**\n- [ ] Confirmation page identifies the record clearly\n- [ ] Primary action is clearly destructive\n- [ ] Cancel link returns to a safe page\n- [ ] Layout matches the rest of the app\n\n### 4. Add Controller Actions\nAdd remove actions to the relevant controller, usually `PlayersController` or `CharactersController`.\n\n**GET Remove/Delete**\n- Load the entity by id\n- If the entity does not exist, return `NotFound()`\n- If the entity is already removed, either return `NotFound()` or redirect to a sensible page based on existing app behavior\n- Return the confirmation view with the entity or a small view model\n\n**POST Remove/Delete**\n- Accept the entity id or a minimal view model\n- Re-check that the entity exists and is not already removed\n- Set `DeletedAt` to the current timestamp\n- Save changes through the repository\n- Redirect to `Index` or another appropriate list page\n\n**Checklist:**\n- [ ] GET action loads the target entity\n- [ ] POST action performs soft delete\n- [ ] Missing entities are handled safely\n- [ ] Success redirects to the right page\n\n### 5. Filter All List Pages\n- Update every list query so it only returns rows where `DeletedAt == null`\n- Apply the filter in controller actions, repository methods, or shared query helpers so it is consistent everywhere\n- Make sure search, paging, and related list features also honor the same filter\n- If admin or maintenance views need to see removed records, keep those separate and explicit\n\n**Checklist:**\n- [ ] Index/list pages exclude removed entities\n- [ ] Filtering is applied consistently across all entity lists\n- [ ] Search and paging do not reintroduce removed records\n\n## Decision Points\n\n**Should the page be called Remove or Delete?**\n- Follow the naming already used in the controller and views\n- If the project already uses `Delete`, keep that route and view name\n- If the UI language should feel softer, use `Remove` in labels while keeping route names aligned with the codebase\n\n**Should removed entities be recoverable?**\n- If yes, keep the `DeletedAt` field and add a future restore workflow\n- If no, still avoid hard delete unless the user explicitly requests permanent deletion\n- For this project, default to soft delete only\n\n**Should details pages show removed entities?**\n- Usually no, because the list views are filtered and the object is treated as inactive\n- If a detail page can still be reached directly, show a not found message or a removed-state message consistently\n\n## Template Pattern\n\n### Entity Pattern\n```csharp\npublic class Player\n{\n    public int PlayerId { get; set; }\n    public string Name { get; set; } = string.Empty;\n    public string Surname { get; set; } = string.Empty;\n    public DateTime? DeletedAt { get; set; }\n}\n```\n\n### Repository Pattern\n```csharp\npublic Player? GetPlayerById(int id)\n{\n    return _context.Players\n        .FirstOrDefault(player => player.PlayerId == id && player.DeletedAt == null);\n}\n\npublic void SoftDeletePlayer(int id)\n{\n    var player = _context.Players.FirstOrDefault(player => player.PlayerId == id && player.DeletedAt == null);\n    if (player == null)\n    {\n        return;\n    }\n\n    player.DeletedAt = DateTime.UtcNow;\n    _context.SaveChanges();\n}\n```\n\n### Controller Pattern\n```csharp\n[HttpGet]\n[Route(\"Delete/{id}\")]\npublic IActionResult Delete(int id)\n{\n    var player = _playerRepository.GetPlayerById(id);\n    if (player == null)\n    {\n        return NotFound();\n    }\n\n    return View(player);\n}\n\n[HttpPost]\n[ValidateAntiForgeryToken]\n[Route(\"Delete/{id}\")]\npublic IActionResult DeleteConfirmed(int id)\n{\n    _playerRepository.SoftDeletePlayer(id);\n    return RedirectToAction(\"Index\");\n}\n```\n\n### View Pattern\n```html\n@model Player\n\n@{\n    ViewData[\"Title\"] = \"Remove Player\";\n}\n\n<section class=\"sheet-card mb-4 reveal\">\n    <p class=\"sheet-kicker mb-1\">Confirm Removal</p>\n    <h1 class=\"h3 mb-1\">@ViewData[\"Title\"]</h1>\n    <p class=\"text-muted mb-0\">This will hide the record from normal lists.</p>\n</section>\n\n<div class=\"row justify-content-center\">\n    <div class=\"col-12 col-lg-8\">\n        <div class=\"card sheet-card reveal mb-4\">\n            <div class=\"card-body\">\n                <dl class=\"row mb-4\">\n                    <dt class=\"col-sm-4\">Name</dt>\n                    <dd class=\"col-sm-8\">@Model.Name</dd>\n                </dl>\n\n                <form asp-action=\"Delete\" asp-route-id=\"@Model.PlayerId\" method=\"post\">\n                    @Html.AntiForgeryToken()\n                    <div class=\"d-flex gap-2\">\n                        <button type=\"submit\" class=\"btn btn-danger\">Remove</button>\n                        <a asp-action=\"Index\" class=\"btn btn-outline-ink\">Cancel</a>\n                    </div>\n                </form>\n            </div>\n        </div>\n    </div>\n</div>\n```\n\n## Quality Checklist\n\n- [ ] Entity includes nullable `DeletedAt`\n- [ ] Delete operation writes the timestamp instead of removing the row\n- [ ] All normal lists filter to `DeletedAt == null`\n- [ ] Confirmation page is clear and matches site styling\n- [ ] Controller handles missing or already removed entities safely\n- [ ] Repository methods stay consistent across the app\n- [ ] Migration and model snapshot are updated when needed\n\n## When updating lists\n- After adding `DeletedAt`, audit all index/list queries in controllers and repositories\n- Make sure the same filter applies to lookup methods used by dropdowns, related data, and search results\n- If a view should intentionally show removed records, make that behavior explicit instead of relying on default queries\n"},"tool_use_id":"call_KNuY4LiOj6N4Pt6GYlbnVJRX__vscode-1779312059816","cwd":"c:\\TVZ\\Druga_apsolventska\\ASP_Net\\Projekt"}

{"timestamp":"2026-05-21T01:46:20.586Z","hook_event_name":"PreToolUse","session_id":"9aad496a-c7c5-4531-9a3e-1dafcaecb5a9","transcript_path":"c:\\Users\\David Laptop\\AppData\\Roaming\\Code\\User\\workspaceStorage\\67336a66a4a40a4161dc2c41d57a767b\\GitHub.copilot-chat\\transcripts\\9aad496a-c7c5-4531-9a3e-1dafcaecb5a9.jsonl","tool_name":"apply_patch","tool_input":{"explanation":"Add a reusable workspace skill for AJAX list search and autocomplete across list-bearing MVC views.","input":"*** Begin Patch\n*** Add File: c:\\TVZ\\Druga_apsolventska\\ASP_Net\\Projekt\\DnD_Character_Sheet_Creator\\.github\\skills\\ajax-list-search\\SKILL.md\n+---\n+name: ajax-list-search\n+description: \"Use when adding or updating search on any list view in the DnD Character Sheet Creator ASP.NET MVC app, especially AJAX filtering, autocomplete dropdowns, and nested lists such as Characters inside Player details.\"\n+---\n+\n+# AJAX List Search Skill\n+\n+Use this skill when a view renders a list of rows, cards, or related child items and needs live search with AJAX-backed autocomplete. The target patterns in this project are pages like Players Index, Characters Index, and nested lists such as the Characters section inside Player Details.\n+\n+## Workflow\n+\n+### 1. Identify the List Surface\n+- Find every view that renders a repeatable list of entities or child entities.\n+- Decide whether the search applies to the page root list, a nested list, or both.\n+- For detail pages, search only the child collection section unless the user explicitly asks for parent filtering too.\n+\n+**Checklist:**\n+- [ ] The searchable list is clearly identified\n+- [ ] Nested lists are treated separately from the main entity details\n+- [ ] Searchable fields are known before editing code\n+\n+### 2. Define the Search Behavior\n+- Use AJAX for both filtering and autocomplete retrieval.\n+- Prefer a dedicated autocomplete endpoint that returns lightweight JSON results.\n+- Prefer a separate list-search endpoint or partial-result endpoint when the visible list must update without a full page reload.\n+- Search should feel live, but should not fire on every keystroke without debounce.\n+\n+**Checklist:**\n+- [ ] Autocomplete has its own AJAX endpoint\n+- [ ] List filtering has a clear server endpoint\n+- [ ] Requests are debounced or otherwise rate-limited\n+\n+### 3. Add Repository Filtering\n+- Add or extend repository methods so the query layer can filter by search text.\n+- Keep the filtering logic server-side so autocomplete and list results use the same rules.\n+- If the list is nested, include the parent key in the query, such as `playerId` for a player's characters.\n+- Return only the fields needed for the autocomplete dropdown when possible.\n+\n+**Checklist:**\n+- [ ] Repository exposes a search-aware method\n+- [ ] Parent-scoped lists keep their parent filter\n+- [ ] Autocomplete data is lightweight\n+\n+### 4. Add Controller Endpoints\n+- Add a GET endpoint for filtered list results.\n+- Add a GET endpoint for autocomplete suggestions, usually returning JSON.\n+- Accept a search term parameter such as `query`, `term`, or `search` and keep the naming consistent within the controller.\n+- If the page is a nested list inside details, keep the parent id parameter in both the list and autocomplete endpoints.\n+\n+**Checklist:**\n+- [ ] List endpoint returns filtered data\n+- [ ] Autocomplete endpoint returns JSON\n+- [ ] Empty or short terms are handled safely\n+\n+### 5. Update the Razor View\n+- Add a visible search box above the list.\n+- Add an autocomplete dropdown or suggestion panel bound to the search input.\n+- Add a placeholder and short helper text when that improves discoverability.\n+- For nested lists in details views, place the search box directly above the child collection section.\n+- Keep the existing card and sheet styling intact.\n+\n+**Checklist:**\n+- [ ] Search UI is easy to find\n+- [ ] Dropdown suggestions are visible and usable\n+- [ ] Nested list search stays near the list it filters\n+\n+### 6. Wire the AJAX Script\n+- Use jQuery AJAX when fitting this app's existing layout and script stack.\n+- Fetch autocomplete results as the user types.\n+- Update the list area when a suggestion is selected or when search is submitted.\n+- Hide the dropdown when the input is empty or when the user clicks outside.\n+- Avoid stale updates by ignoring older responses when a newer request has already started.\n+\n+**Checklist:**\n+- [ ] Typing loads suggestions asynchronously\n+- [ ] Selecting a suggestion updates the list or navigates to the chosen record\n+- [ ] Stale AJAX responses do not overwrite newer results\n+\n+### 7. Verify the Experience\n+- Confirm the list still renders correctly with no search term.\n+- Confirm search works after clearing the field.\n+- Confirm autocomplete suggestions match the same server-side rules as the filtered list.\n+- Confirm the nested Characters list in Player Details only searches that section and not the whole player record.\n+\n+**Checklist:**\n+- [ ] Default list state still works\n+- [ ] Search returns expected matches\n+- [ ] Autocomplete and list filtering agree\n+- [ ] Nested lists stay scoped correctly\n+\n+## Decision Points\n+\n+**Should search reload the whole page or only the list area?**\n+- Prefer only the list area when the page is a list-first screen.\n+- Prefer a full-page navigation only if the page already uses standard query-string filtering and the list is simple.\n+- For this project, default to AJAX list replacement.\n+\n+**Should autocomplete return full records or labels only?**\n+- Return minimal JSON needed to render the dropdown.\n+- Include the id plus the display label, and add any secondary text only if the UI needs it.\n+\n+**Should nested lists reuse the same endpoint as the main index page?**\n+- Only when the search scope and fields are identical.\n+- Otherwise keep a dedicated endpoint so the parent entity and the child collection do not interfere with each other.\n+\n+## Template Pattern\n+\n+### Controller Pattern\n+```csharp\n+[HttpGet]\n+public IActionResult Search(string? query)\n+{\n+    var results = _playerRepository.SearchPlayers(query);\n+    return PartialView(\"_PlayerList\", results);\n+}\n+\n+[HttpGet]\n+public IActionResult Autocomplete(string term)\n+{\n+    var suggestions = _playerRepository.SearchPlayers(term)\n+        .Select(player => new\n+        {\n+            id = player.PlayerId,\n+            label = player.Name + \" \" + player.Surname\n+        })\n+        .ToList();\n+\n+    return Json(suggestions);\n+}\n+```\n+\n+### Razor View Pattern\n+```html\n+<div class=\"sheet-card mb-4 reveal\">\n+    <div class=\"row g-3 align-items-end\">\n+        <div class=\"col-12 col-lg-8\">\n+            <label for=\"searchInput\" class=\"form-label\">Search</label>\n+            <input id=\"searchInput\" class=\"form-control\" type=\"search\" placeholder=\"Search players...\" autocomplete=\"off\" />\n+            <div id=\"autocompletePanel\" class=\"list-group position-absolute w-100 d-none\"></div>\n+        </div>\n+    </div>\n+</div>\n+\n+<div id=\"listRegion\">\n+    @await Html.PartialAsync(\"_PlayerList\", Model)\n+</div>\n+```\n+\n+### AJAX Pattern\n+```javascript\n+let activeRequest = null;\n+\n+$('#searchInput').on('input', function () {\n+    const term = $(this).val();\n+\n+    if (activeRequest) {\n+        activeRequest.abort();\n+    }\n+\n+    activeRequest = $.ajax({\n+        url: '/Players/Autocomplete',\n+        data: { term: term },\n+        success: function (items) {\n+ Rendering (display-only): use a shared Razor partial that emits an ISO timestamp and a fallback text.\n   - Partial example: `Views/Shared/_DateTimeControl.cshtml` accepts a `DateTime?` and renders `<time data-date-time-control=\"true\" data-date-time-iso=\"...\">fallback</time>`.\n2. Client formatting: use `Intl.DateTimeFormat` with the browser locale.\n   - Example JS snippet:\n     ```js\n     const locale = (navigator.languages && navigator.languages[0]) || navigator.language || document.documentElement.lang || 'en-US';\n     const fmt = new Intl.DateTimeFormat(locale, { dateStyle: 'medium', timeStyle: 'short' });\n     const dt = new Date(isoString);\n     element.textContent = fmt.format(dt);\n     ```\n3. | Foreign Key to Player |\n| OIB | string | Croatian ID |\n| JMBG | string | Birth number |\n\n**Roles:** \n- Admin (full access)\n- Manager (can manage players/characters)\n\n---\n\n#### Character\nRepresents a D&D character created by a player.\n\n| Property | Type | Notes |\n|----------|------|-------|\n| CharacterId | int | Primary Key |\n| CharacterName | string | Required |\n| PlayerId | int | Foreign Key |\n| Class | ClassEnum | Barbarian, Bard, Cleric, etc. |\n| Race | RaceEnum | Human, Elf, Dwarf, etc. |\n| Background | BackgroundEnum | Acolyte, Criminal, etc. |\n| Alignment | AlignmentEnum | LawfulGood, ChaoticEvil, etc. |\n| LevelId | int? | Foreign Key to CharacterLevel |\n| DeletedAt | DateTime? | Soft-delete marker |\n\n**Relationships:**\n- Many Characters GĺĆ One Player\n- Many Characters GĺĆ One CharacterLevel\n- One Character GĺĆ Many Equipment\n- One Character GĺĆ Many Attachments\n\n---\n\n#### CharacterLevel\nRepresents experience and level progression for a character.\n\n| Property | Type | Notes |\n|----------|------|-------|\n| LevelId | int | Primary Key |\n| LevelNumber | int | 1-20 |\n| Experience | int | XP total |\n| HitPoints | int | Max HP |\n\n**Relationships:**\n- Many CharacterLevels GĺÉ Many Characters\n\n---\n\n#### Equipment\nRepresents items carried by a character (weapons, armor, gear).\n\n| Property | Type | Notes |\n|----------|------|-------|\n| EquipmentId | int | Primary Key |\n| CharacterId | int | Foreign Key |\n| Equipment_Type | EquipmentType | Weapon, Armour, AdventuringGear, Tools |\n| Equipment_Name | string | Item name |\n| Cost | decimal | Gold pieces |\n| Weight | decimal | Pounds |\n| DeletedAt | DateTime? | Soft-delete marker |\n\n**Derived/Domain Classes:**\n- Weapon (extends Equipment with DamageAmount, DamageType, WeaponPropertiesEnum)\n- Armour (extends Equipment with armor class info)\n- Tools, AdventuringGear (specialized equipment types)\n\n---\n\n#### Attachment\nFile attachments uploaded to a character (images, documents, notes).\n\n| Property | Type | Notes |\n|----------|------|-------|\n| AttachmentId | int | Primary Key |\n| CharacterId | int | Foreign Key |\n| FileName | string | Original filename |\n| FilePath | string | Server path (wwwroot/uploads/characters/{id}/) |\n| DateUploaded | DateTime | Upload timestamp |\n| DeletedAt | DateTime? | Soft-delete marker |\n\n**Relationships:**\n- Many Attachments GĺÉ One Character\n\n---\n\n### Supporting Enums\n\n| Enum | Values |\n|------|--------|\n| ClassEnum | Barbarian, Bard, Cleric, Druid, Fighter, Monk, Paladin, Ranger, Rogue, Sorcerer, Warlock, Wizard |\n| RaceEnum | Human, Elf, Dwarf, Halfling, Tiefling, Dragonborn, Gnome |\n| BackgroundEnum | Acolyte, Charlatan, Criminal, Entertainer, Folk Hero, Guild Artisan, Hermit, Noble, Outlander, Sage, Sailor, Soldier, Urchin |\n| AlignmentEnum | LawfulGood, NeutralGood, ChaoticGood, LawfulNeutral, TrueNeutral, ChaoticNeutral, LawfulEvil, NeutralEvil, ChaoticEvil |\n| WeaponPropertiesEnum | Ammunition, Finesse, Heavy, Light, Loading, Range, Reach, Special, Thrown, TwoHanded, Versatile |\n\n---\n\n## Authentication & Authorization\n\n### Authentication Flow\n1. Internal Server Error** - unhandled exception\n\n---\n\n## Notes for Developers\n\n1. **Soft Delete**: All list endpoints exclude deleted records (DeletedAt != null)\n2. **Identity Integration**: List[PlayerSummaryDto] | Fetch all players (paginated) |\n| /api/players/{id} | GET | 200/404 | PlayerDto | Fetch by ID |\n| /api/players | POST | 201 | PlayerDto | Create new player |\n| /api/players/{id} | PUT | 200/204 | PlayerDto | Update player |\n| /api/players/{id} | DELETE | 200/204 | - | Soft-delete player |\n\n**Route:** `/api/players`  \n**Controller:** `PlayersApiController`  \n**DTO Classes:** `PlayerUpsertDto`, `PlayerDto`, `PlayerSummaryDto`\n\n---\n\n### Characters API\n\n| Route | Method | Status | Response | Notes |\n|-------|--------|--------|----------|-------|\n| /api/characters | GET | 200 | List[CharacterSummaryDto] | Fetch all characters |\n| /api/characters/{id} | GET | 200/404 | CharacterDto | Fetch by ID with level/equipment |\n| /api/characters | POST | 201 | CharacterDto | Create with auto-generated level |\n| /api/characters/{id} | PUT | 200/204 | CharacterDto | Update character |\n| /api/characters/{id} | DELETE | 200/204 | - | Soft-delete character |\n\n**Route:** `/api/characters`  \n**Controller:** `CharactersApiController`  \n**DTO Classes:** `CharacterUpsertDto`, `CharacterDto`, `CharacterSummaryDto`\n\n---\n\n### Equipment API\n\n| Route | Method | Status | Response | Notes |\n|-------|--------|--------|----------|-------|\n| /api/equipment | GET | 200 | (configured in `ApiController` base or method attributes):\n\n- **Public GET endpoints** (read-only): Accessible to authenticated users\n- **POST/PUT/DELETE endpoints**: Require `[Authorize(Roles = \"Admin,Manager\")]`\n- **Character attachments/equipment**: Require authentication\n\n---\n\n## Data Files & Uploads\n\n**File Upload Location:**\n- Physical path: `wwwroot/uploads/characters/{characterId}/`\n- Metadata stored in `Attachment` entity\n- Soft-delete supported\n\n---\n\n## Error Handling\n\n- **400 Bad Request** - validation errors (ModelState invalid)\n- **401 Unauthorized** - user not authenticated\n- **403 Forbidden** - user lacks required role/permission\n- **404 Not Found** - resource doesn't exist or is deleted\n- **500 Internal Server Error** - unhandled exception\n\n---\n\n## Notes for Developers\n\n1. **Soft Delete**: All list endpoints exclude deleted records (DeletedAt != null)\n2. **Identity Integration**: Player record linked to AppUser via PlayerId\n3. **File Uploads**: Stored server-side; metadata in database\n4. **CORS**: Not enabled; internal app only\n5. **API Versioning**: Not currently implemented; all APIs
